title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
How to Open an E-Insurance Account?
As the digital world strengthens its hold, several industries and companies are now providing online offerings to their customers. Access to internet connectivity and advanced cell phones has also increased for Indians. This is helping more and more people make well-informed decisions and choose more convenient options in various regards.
A part of this virtual change is the emergence of e-insurance accounts.
Within the life insurance industry, the concept of e-insurance has gained prominent attention as the awareness of the need for accessible and well-designed insurance policies has grown. For the uninitiated, e-insurance refers to the buying and selling, and more importantly, the storage of insurance via an electronic platform.
As a policyholder, an e-insurance account (eIA) can benefit you in many ways. Wondering what exactly is an e-insurance account and how to open one? Here’s a guide to help you out.
What is an e-insurance account?
An electronic insurance account is an electronic storage facility where you can store all your insurance policies. The policies held therein are referred to as dematerialised policies. The most beneficial feature of an e-insurance account is that one can access all of their insurance policies within a single platform, in a matter of a few clicks.
Each e-insurance account is connected to a unique number, also called the eIA number. Once this number has been created, it cannot be altered or modified. Further, during the registration of an electronic insurance account, one must provide KYC details. When buying a new life insurance policy, one must enter the eIA number when filing their proposal form. This eliminates the need to provide KYC details with every purchase.
Presently, the following four entities1 have been authorised with the certificate:
NSDL Database Management Limited
Central Insurance Repository Limited
Karvy Insurance Repository Limited
CAMS Repository Services Limited
How to open an e-insurance account2?
The process of how to open an e-insurance account is a simple one, comprising three steps, which are:
Step 1: Download the eIA form from the insurance repository
As mentioned earlier, currently, 4 insurance repositories are providing services for opening an e-insurance account. You can choose your preferred repository to download the eIA application form. Alternatively, you can also fill out the account opening form via the website of the repository itself.
Step 2: Fill out the eIA form and attach KYC
Fill out the eIA form, correctly mentioning your address, correspondence and contact details, bank account information, and so on. You will also have to mention the details of the person you wish to designate as the Authorised Representative. In the event of the account holder’s demise or incapacity to access their account, the Authorised Representative can access the e-insurance account on their behalf.
Once the required details are filled out, ensure to attach the required KYC documents (a list of the same is provided later ahead).
Step 3: Submit the form
The last step in how to open an eIA account includes the physical submission of the filled-out form. You can submit the form via an Approved Person (attached to the insurance repository) or the insurance company selling you the policy.
The insurance repository will then coordinate with you and provide your eIA number alongside the login ID and the process to set your password. Once you choose a password, you can access your e-insurance account.
What documents are required3?
The process of how to open an eIA account is incomplete unless you have submitted the following documents:
Proof of identity
Proof of address
Proof of date of birth
PAN card
When submitting the eIA opening form to the Approved Person of the repository, you may have to carry along with you the physical copies of the given documents.
Benefits of opening an e-insurance account4
Once you know how to open an e-insurance account, you should also know the benefits of having one. Opening an e-insurance account can ease your insurance buying and managing journey to a great extent. Some of the key benefits you can enjoy are:
Access to all policies
With one e-insurance account, you can access all your insurance policies in one place. It makes the several processes related to insurance, such as renewal payment and porting policies, relatively easy.
Keeps them safe
Storing your insurance policies in your eIA also means that they are safe from physical theft or loss. The repository provides secure payment gateways that ensure the data you enter is inaccessible to unethical parties.
Timely reminders
Having an e-insurance account helps you be more timely with your renewal since you shall receive timely reminders for the same. This also ensures to a certain extent that your policy coverage stays consistent and does not lapse due to a late premium payment.
A life insurance policy can be a significant aspect of living a financially secure life. The emergence of e-insurance can help old and new life insurance policyholders have a more comfortable and convenient experience with their insurance journeys.
Reference Links:
1https://www.policyx.com/health-insurance/articles/electronic-insurance-account/#:~:text=How%20do%20you%20open,Karvy%20Insurance%20Repository%20Limited
2https://nir.ndml.in/pdf/ndml-form-opt2.pdf
3https://nir.ndml.in/e-insurance-account-opening-policy-conversion-process.html#:~:text=KYC%20Documents%20required%20while%20submitting%20the%20e%2DInsurance%20Account%20application%20form
4https://www.policyx.com/health-insurance/articles/electronic-insurance-account/
full_pdf_text:
How to Open an e-Insurance Account? The insurance industry is beginning to feel the digital effect1. It is changing how products and services are delivered, and increasingly it will change the nature of those products and services and even the business model itself1. An insurance repository system was formally launched on 16th September 2013. It enables policyholders to keep all their insurance policies in electronic form. This also facilitates undertaking changes, modifications and revisions in the insurance policy efficiently. The repository system also brings about efficiency, cost reduction and transparency in the issuance and maintenance of insurance policies. What is an e-Insurance Account? An electronic insurance account is an electronic account opened by a person with an insurance repository wherein his portfolios of insurance policies are held in an electronic form. The most beneficial feature of an e-insurance account is that one can access all of their insurance policies at a single place, in a matter of a few clicks. Each e-insurance account is connected to a unique number, also called the eIA number. Once this number has been created, it cannot be altered or modified. Further, during the registration of an electronic insurance account, one must provide KYC details. When buying a new life insurance policy, one must enter the eIA number when filing their proposal form. This eliminates the need to provide KYC details with every purchase. Presently, the following four entities2 have been authorised to act as Insurance repositories:
 NSDL Database Management Limited  Central Insurance Repository Limited  Karvy Insurance Repository Limited  CAMS Repository Services Limited How to Open an e-Insurance Account3? The process of how to open an e-insurance account is a simple one, comprising two steps, which are:
Step 1: Fill out the eIA form and attach KYC Visit the repository’s website to download the form. Fill out the eIA form, correctly mentioning your address, contact details, bank account information, and so on. You will also have to mention the details of the person you wish to designate as the Authorised Representative. Once all the details are filled out, attach the required KYC documents.
Step 2: Submit the form The next step in how to open an eIA account includes the submission of the filled-out form. If applying online, you can submit it online itself on the repository’s website. For offline mode, you can submit the form via an Approved Person (appointed by the insurance repository). The repository will then verify the details and open your e-insurance account. The insurance repository will then provide you the welcome kit, alongside the login credentials for accessing your account. KYC Documents Required4 The process of how to open an eIA account is incomplete unless you have submitted the KYC documents like identity proof, address proof, proof of date of birth, among others. You can visit the insurance repository’s website for a detailed list of the acceptable KYC documents under each category. Benefits of Opening an e-Insurance Account5 Once you know about an e-insurance account, you should also know the benefits of having one. Opening an e-insurance account can ease your insurance buying and managing journey to a great extent. Some of the key benefits you can enjoy are:  Access to all policies
With one e-insurance account, you can access all your insurance policies in one place. It makes the processes related to policy servicing, relatively easy.  Keeps them safe
Storing your insurance policies in your eIA also means that they are safe from physical theft or loss. The repository provides secure payment gateways that ensure the data you enter is inaccessible to unethical parties.  Easy Service
You can easily make changes to details like addresses, contact details, etc. for all your policies through eIA.  Premium Payments
eIA also facilitates premium payment online for your policies. A life insurance policy can be a significant aspect of living a financially secure life. The emergence of e-insurance can help old and new life insurance policyholders have a more comfortable and convenient experience with their insurance journeys. Reference Links:
1https://www.mckinsey.com/~/media/mckinsey/industries/financial%20services/our %20insights/time%20for%20insurance%20companies%20to%20face%20digital%20re ality/digital-disruption-in-insurance.ashx 2https://www.irdai.gov.in/ADMINCMS/cms/NormalData_Layout.aspx?page=PageNo 2054&mid=9.7.1 3https://nir.ndml.in/e-insurance-account-opening-policy-conversion-process.html 4https://nir.ndml.in/e-insurance-account-opening-policy-conversion-process.html 5https://www.insurancedekho.com/health-insurance/articles/everything-you- should-know-about-e-insurance-accounts-121
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
